title: Universal plant and animal eDNA metabarcoding using Nanopore sequencing
author: Lucas Esteban Wange
materials: Discard the supernatant using a P1000 pipette
Air-dry beads for 5 min on the magnet.
Typically we aim for 300 fmol total input
Start the BioScanRT pipeline as sepcified in github

steps.title: Add 1,040 µl Proteinase K Storage...
steps.instruction: Add 1,040 µl Proteinase K Storage Buffer to each Proteinase K (20 mg) tube prior to use. Store at -20ºC after mixing.

steps.title: Add up to 50 mg material...
steps.instruction: Add up to 50 mg material to a bead beating tube containing DNA/RNA Shield and mechanically homogenize your sample on a vortex mixer at maximum speed for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add 20 µl Proteinase K and mix well
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl Proteinase K and mix well. Incubate at RT for 30 min.l

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 10,000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 10,000 x g for 1 min to pellet the debris and transfer up to 400 µl of lysate into a new tube.

steps.title: Add 400 µl Quick-DNA MagBinding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl Quick-DNA MagBinding Buffer to 400 µl digested sample and mix by pipetting or inverting 10 times.

steps.title: Add 33 µl of MagBinding Beads to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 33 µl of MagBinding Beads to each sample. If using a repeater pipett use 30 µl

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the magnetic rack, wait until beads have separated from the solution

steps.title: Transfer the sample off the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample off the magnetic rack, add 500 µl Quick-DNA MagBinding Buffer.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the magnetic rack, wait until beads have separated from the solution and discard the supernatant using a P1000 pipette

steps.title: Transfer the sample off the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample off the magnetic rack, add 500 µl DNA Pre-Wash Buffer and mix the sample by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the magnetic rack, wait until beads have separated from the solution and discard the supernatant using a P1000 pipette

steps.title: Transfer the sample off the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample off the magnetic rack, add 900 µl g-DNA Wash Buffer and mix the sample by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the magnetic rack, wait until beads have separated from the solution and discard the supernatant using a P1000 pipette

steps.title: Repeat steps 15-16 two more times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 15-16 two more times.

steps.title: Add 40 µl of DNA Elution Buffer to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of DNA Elution Buffer to each sample.

steps.title: Transfer the sample to the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Transfer the sample to the magnetic rack, wait until beads have separated from the solution and transfer the eluted DNA to a new tube.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 µl of forward reverse barcode mix per well. Or 1.75 µl forward mix and 1.75 µl reverse mix when not using pre-mixed primers. See Notes in "Before you start" section Use each forward/reverse barcode combination just once per experiment. This is crucial to be able to demultiplex the data from different wells. Note down which primer combination was used for each well meticulously

steps.title: Incubate as indicated below A B...
steps.instruction: Incubate as indicated below A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Repeat 2 Initial denaturation 95 ºC 15m 3 denaturation 95 ºC 30s 35 cycles 4 annealing 55 ºC 1m 30s 5 extend 72 ºC 1m 30s 6 final extension 72 ºC 15m 7 store 12 ºC Inf Multiplex PCR cycling conditions

steps.title: Choose one normalisation strategy here
steps.instruction: Choose one normalisation strategy here. Concentration based normalisation gives you more information about the PCR amplification success for each sample, while Bead based normalisation is faster, cheaper and more scalable .

steps.title: For each library make a master mix
steps.instruction: For each library make a master mix: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Klenow fragment exo- (5 U/µl) 1.5 µl 3 T4 PNK (10 U/µl) 2 µl 4 dATP (10mM) 0.5 µl 5 T4 DNA ligase buffer (10x) 4 µl 6 Reaction booster 2 µl 7 Total 10 µl

steps.title: Add 10 µl of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µl of master mix to each 30 µl amplicon pool, mix by pipetting and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate as indicated below
steps.instruction: Incubate as indicated below: A B 1 Temperature Time 2 37 ºC 30m 3 65 ºC 5m 4 4 ºC inf

steps.title: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA)...
steps.instruction: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA) and Quick T4 Ligase, and place on ice.

steps.title: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB), Elution Buffer...
steps.instruction: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB), Elution Buffer (EB) and Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at RT and place on ice immediately after thawing and mixing.

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tube, mix in the following order: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Endrepaired Amplicon pool 60 µl 3 Ligation Buffer (LNB) 25 µl 4 NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 10 µl 5 Ligation Adapter (LA) 5 µl 6 Total 100 µl

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting and briefly spinning down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP)
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP).

steps.title: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Spin down the sample, place it...
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample, place it on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant when it is clear.

steps.title: Wash the beads twice with 250...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads twice with 250 µl Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Spin down, place the tube back...
steps.instruction: Spin down, place the tube back on the magnet and pipette off any residual supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µl Elution Buffer (EB).

steps.title: Pellet the beads on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on the magnet until the eluate is clear.

steps.title: Remove 15 µl of eluate and...
steps.instruction: Remove 15 µl of eluate and perform concentration measurement using Qubit HS DNA kit according to manufacturers instructions.

steps.title: Prepare 50 fmol in 12 µl of Elution Buffer (EB)
steps.instruction: Prepare 50 fmol in 12 µl of Elution Buffer (EB).

steps.title: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library Beads (LIB), Flow Cell Tether (FCT) and Flow Cell Flush (FCF) at RT and place on ice after thawing.

steps.title: To prepare the flow cell priming...
steps.instruction: To prepare the flow cell priming mix with BSA, combine the following reagents: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Flow Cell Flush (FCF) 1170 µl 3 Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) at 50 mg/ml 5 µl 4 Flow Cell Tether (FCT) 30 µl

steps.title: Open the MinION device lid, slide...
steps.instruction: Open the MinION device lid, slide the flow cell under the clip and press down firmly on the flow cell.

steps.title: Slide the flow cell priming port...
steps.instruction: Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubbles:

steps.title: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl
steps.instruction: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl.

steps.title: Insert the tip into the priming port
steps.instruction: Insert the tip into the priming port.

steps.title: Turn the wheel until the dial...
steps.instruction: Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µl, to draw back 20-30 µl or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip.

steps.title: Load 800 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port and wait for 5 min.

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Sequencing Buffer (SB) 37.5 µl 3 Library Beads (LB) 25.5 µl 4 DNA library (50 fmol) 12 µl

steps.title: Gently lift the SpotON simple port...
steps.instruction: Gently lift the SpotON simple port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible.

steps.title: Load 200 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 200 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Add 75 µl of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 75 µl of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port and close the priming port.

steps.title: Place the light shield onto the flow cell
steps.instruction: Place the light shield onto the flow cell.

steps.title: Mix by shaking for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Mix by shaking for 10 min at 1100 – 1500 rpm or by inverting regularly

steps.title: Mix by shaking or inverting regularly for 10 min
steps.instruction: Mix by shaking or inverting regularly for 10 min

steps.title: Mix by shaking at RT for 5 min
steps.instruction: Mix by shaking at RT for 5 min.

steps.title: Add 19
steps.instruction: Add 19.5 µl eDNA to one well per sample (6.5 per technical triplicate)

steps.title: Add 30µl 2x Qiagen Multiplex PCR mix to each well
steps.instruction: Add 30µl 2x Qiagen Multiplex PCR mix to each well

steps.title: Mix sample and PCR mastermix with...
steps.instruction: Mix sample and PCR mastermix with a multichanel pipette and distribute 16.5 µl into a total of 3 wells per sample.

steps.title: When using a microwell plate, seal...
steps.instruction: When using a microwell plate, seal very well to avoid cross contamination. We recommend using thick plastic seals like MicroAmp clear Adhesive Film

steps.title: Set up a no template control...
steps.instruction: Set up a no template control reaction (NTC) and if used a positive control containing high quality DNA of animals and/or plants NTC: 10µl 2x Qiagen Multiplex PCR mix, 6.5 µl H2O Positive Control: 10µl 2x Qiagen Multiplex PCR mix, 6.5 µl diluted control DNA (max. 1ng total)

steps.title: Equilibrate SPRI bead solution to RT...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate SPRI bead solution to RT and vortex until the solution appears homogeneous.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5x volume beads (with 60 µl of sample, add 90 µl of beads).

steps.title: Mix 10 times with a pipette...
steps.instruction: Mix 10 times with a pipette and incubate for 5 min.

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnet and leave it until the supernatant has cleared.

steps.title: Wash twice in 100 µl freshly prepared 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Wash twice in 100 µl freshly prepared 80% EtOH.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the magnet and add 30 µl ddH2O to the beads.

steps.title: Mix 10 times with a multichannel...
steps.instruction: Mix 10 times with a multichannel pipette and incubate for 5 min.

steps.title: Put back on the magnet until...
steps.instruction: Put back on the magnet until the supernatant has cleared and transfer the DNA to a new tube.

steps.title: measure 1µl of the cleaned up...
steps.instruction: measure 1µl of the cleaned up PCR product using a Qubit flourometer and the Qubit HS DNA Kit according to manufacturers instructions.

steps.title: In a spreadsheet calculate the amount...
steps.instruction: In a spreadsheet calculate the amount of DNA to add for each sample. We asume here that the amplicons will have a similar length distribution in all samples.

steps.title: Add normalisation solution at a ratio...
steps.instruction: Add normalisation solution at a ratio of 1:1 to each sample (for 20µl PCR, add 20 µl normalisation solution)

steps.title: Measure concentration of the pooled samples...
steps.instruction: Measure concentration of the pooled samples , the NTC and the positive control if available using Qubit High Sensitivity dsDNA following the manufacturers instructions

steps.title: Add isopropanol at a ratio of...
steps.instruction: Add isopropanol at a ratio of 1:1 (for 20µl PCR add 20 µl Isopropanol)

steps.title: Mix the Sample+ Normalisation solution +...
steps.instruction: Mix the Sample+ Normalisation solution + Isopropanol (60µl ototal) thouroughly using pipetting or pulse vortex

steps.title: Incubate for 10 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 min at room temperature

steps.title: Place on a magnet and after...
steps.instruction: Place on a magnet and after solution clears (~5 min) remove supernatant

steps.title: Keep plate on magnet and wash...
steps.instruction: Keep plate on magnet and wash with 75 µl freshly made 80% EtoH

steps.title: Remove all residual Ethanol and air dry for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Remove all residual Ethanol and air dry for 30 seconds

steps.title: Resuspend each row of the plate...
steps.instruction: Resuspend each row of the plate in 50 µl PCR grade H2O

steps.title: Pool all samples apart from the...
steps.instruction: Pool all samples apart from the positive and negative (NTC) controls into one 1.5 ml tube and incubate for 5 minutes. remove positive and NTC controls. if the positive control is not removed but pooled directly it might get too many reads.

steps.title: Place tube on a magnet and...
steps.instruction: Place tube on a magnet and transfer the supernatant to a new tube when clear

steps.title: Pool 3 replicates of each sample...
steps.instruction: Pool 3 replicates of each sample in one tube/well using a multichannel pipette

steps.title: Here we use the Oxford Nanopore...
steps.instruction: Here we use the Oxford Nanopore Library Preparation Kit (Ligation Sequencing Kit V14)

steps.title: Add 20 µl ddH2O to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl ddH2O to the sample.

steps.title: Spin down and incubate for 10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Spin down and incubate for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Calculate molarity using following formula
steps.instruction: Calculate molarity using following formula: average fragment size for a library containing COI and ITS2 is ~500 bp Molarity in nM= concentration (ng/µl)/660*mean fragment length (bp)*1e6 µl for 50 fmol= (50 fmol*660* mean fragment length (bp) *1e6)/concentration (ng/µl)

steps.title: For detailed instructions on how to...
steps.instruction: For detailed instructions on how to load a Minion flow cell consult Nanopores documentation

steps.title: Here we assume you have already...
steps.instruction: Here we assume you have already set up and tested the BioScanRT pipeline following the instructions in github and you have the newest version of MinKnow installed (either on the MinIon device (Mk1c) or on the computer used to control the run (Mk1B or Mk1D)

steps.title: Run the Flow Cell check
steps.instruction: Run the Flow Cell check. This can also be done already before the Priming and Loading section to save time.

steps.title: Start the MinKnow run with standard...
steps.instruction: Start the MinKnow run with standard parameters for the Library Preparation Kit (Ligation Sequencing Kit V14), disable bascalling as this will be done on the Laptop or Macbook, depending on library size (<200bp) select to include small fragments

steps.title: Start the BioScanRT pipeline as outlined...
steps.instruction: Start the BioScanRT pipeline as outlined in the corresponding github repository.

steps.title: For each library make a master mix
steps.instruction: For each library make a master mix: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Klenow fragment exo- (5 U/µl) 1.5 µl 3 T4 PNK (10 U/µl) 2 µl 4 dATP (10mM) 0.5 µl 5 T4 DNA ligase buffer (10x) 4 µl 6 Reaction booster 2 µl 7 Total 10 µl

steps.title: Add 10 µl of master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µl of master mix to each 30 µl amplicon pool, mix by pipetting and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate as indicated below
steps.instruction: Incubate as indicated below: A B 1 Temperature Time 2 37 ºC 30m 3 65 ºC 5m 4 4 ºC inf

steps.instruction: Here we use the Oxford Nanopore Library Preparation Kit (Ligation Sequencing Kit V14)

steps.instruction: Spin down the Ligation Adapter (LA) and Quick T4 Ligase, and place on ice.

steps.title: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB), Elution Buffer...
steps.instruction: Thaw Ligation Buffer (LNB), Elution Buffer (EB) and Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) at RT and place on ice immediately after thawing and mixing.

steps.title: Add 20 µl ddH2O to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl ddH2O to the sample.

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tube, mix in the following order: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Endrepaired Amplicon pool 60 µl 3 Ligation Buffer (LNB) 25 µl 4 NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 10 µl 5 Ligation Adapter (LA) 5 µl 6 Total 100 µl

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting and briefly spinning down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP)
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP).

steps.title: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Spin down the sample, place it...
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample, place it on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant when it is clear.

steps.title: Wash the beads twice with 250...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads twice with 250 µl Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Spin down, place the tube back...
steps.instruction: Spin down, place the tube back on the magnet and pipette off any residual supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µl Elution Buffer (EB).

steps.title: Spin down and incubate for 10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Spin down and incubate for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Pellet the beads on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on the magnet until the eluate is clear.

steps.title: Remove 15 µl of eluate and...
steps.instruction: Remove 15 µl of eluate and perform concentration measurement using Qubit HS DNA kit according to manufacturers instructions.

steps.title: Calculate molarity using following formula
steps.instruction: Calculate molarity using following formula: average fragment size for a library containing COI and ITS2 is ~500 bp Molarity in nM= concentration (ng/µl)/660*mean fragment length (bp)*1e6 µl for 50 fmol= (50 fmol*660* mean fragment length (bp) *1e6)/concentration (ng/µl)

steps.title: Prepare 50 fmol in 12 µl of Elution Buffer (EB)
steps.instruction: Prepare 50 fmol in 12 µl of Elution Buffer (EB).

steps.title: Here we assume you have already...
steps.instruction: Here we assume you have already set up and tested the BioScanRT pipeline following the instructions in github and you have the newest version of MinKnow installed (either on the MinIon device (Mk1c) or on the computer used to control the run (Mk1B or Mk1D)

steps.title: Run the Flow Cell check
steps.instruction: Run the Flow Cell check. This can also be done already before the Priming and Loading section to save time.

steps.title: Start the MinKnow run with standard...
steps.instruction: Start the MinKnow run with standard parameters for the Library Preparation Kit (Ligation Sequencing Kit V14), disable bascalling as this will be done on the Laptop or Macbook, depending on library size (<200bp) select to include small fragments

steps.title: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SB), Library Beads (LIB), Flow Cell Tether (FCT) and Flow Cell Flush (FCF) at RT and place on ice after thawing.

steps.title: To prepare the flow cell priming...
steps.instruction: To prepare the flow cell priming mix with BSA, combine the following reagents: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Flow Cell Flush (FCF) 1170 µl 3 Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) at 50 mg/ml 5 µl 4 Flow Cell Tether (FCT) 30 µl

steps.title: For detailed instructions on how to...
steps.instruction: For detailed instructions on how to load a MinIon flow cell consult Nanopores documentation

steps.title: Open the MinION device lid, slide...
steps.instruction: Open the MinION device lid, slide the flow cell under the clip and press down firmly on the flow cell.

steps.title: Slide the flow cell priming port...
steps.instruction: Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubbles:

steps.title: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl
steps.instruction: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl.

steps.title: Insert the tip into the priming port
steps.instruction: Insert the tip into the priming port.

steps.title: Turn the wheel until the dial...
steps.instruction: Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µl, to draw back 20-30 µl or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip.

steps.title: Load 800 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port and wait for 5 min.

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Sequencing Buffer (SB) 37.5 µl 3 Library Beads (LB) 25.5 µl 4 DNA library (50 fmol) 12 µl

steps.title: Gently lift the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible.

steps.title: Load 200 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 200 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Add 75 µl of the prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 75 µl of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port and close the priming port.

steps.title: Place the light shield onto the flow cell
steps.instruction: Place the light shield onto the flow cell.

steps.title: Calculate input into library preparation
steps.instruction: Calculate input into library preparation. We usually aim to start from 300 fmol but we have generated libraries from less

steps.title: Calculate molarity using following formula
steps.instruction: Calculate molarity using following formula: average fragment size for a library containing COI and ITS2 is ~500 bp Molarity in nM= concentration (ng/µl)/660*mean fragment length (bp)*1e6 µl for 300 fmol= (300 fmol*660* mean fragment length (bp) *1e6)/concentration (ng/µl)

steps.title: Pipette calculated amount of the Amplicon...
steps.instruction: Pipette calculated amount of the Amplicon pool and add PCR grade H2O to 30 µl

steps.title: Calculate the molarities of NTC, positive...
steps.instruction: Calculate the molarities of NTC, positive control and amplicon pool Molarity in nM= concentration (ng/µl)/660*mean fragment length (bp)*1e6 Average fragment size for a library containing COI and ITS2 is ~500 bp

steps.title: Caclulate the average molarity per sample
steps.instruction: Caclulate the average molarity per sample: Library molarity(nmol) / number of samples = x nmol

steps.title: Prepare normalisation solution i
steps.instruction: Prepare normalisation solution i.e. per PCR reaction 1µl SPRI Beads+ 19 µl Bead Binding Buffer. For 96 Samples add 2090 µl Bead Binding Buffer and 110 µl SPRI Beads in a 2 ml Microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Based on the amounts calculated pool...
steps.instruction: Based on the amounts calculated pool amplicons equimolarly and add H2O to 30 µl if necessary.

steps.title: Add x nmol of positive control...
steps.instruction: Add x nmol of positive control add the same volume in µl of the NTC this way the positive control should get approximately the same number of reads as the average sample
